factors.summary: The company encountered difficulties with pricing power as customer rates declined more than transportation costs, affecting net revenue margins and total transportation revenues.

evidence.text: Our Transportation net revenue margin decreased to 17.6 percent in the third quarter of 2016 from 18.4 percent in the third quarter of 2015 primarily due to customer rates decreasing more than costs of transportation, including fuel, in nearly all transportation services.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2015

evidence.text: The decrease in total Transportation revenues was driven by lower rates charged to our customers, including fuel, partially offset by increased volumes in most of our transportation services.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2016 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2015

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining customer rates affected net revenue margins, indicating weakened pricing power.

factors.summary: Overall, volume trends show a mixed performance across transportation services. While most segments experienced increases, certain areas like intermodal saw declines due to competition from lower-cost trucking options.

evidence.text: Truckload volumes increased approximately 7.5 percent in the third quarter of 2016 compared to the third quarter of 2015.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2015

evidence.text: Intermodal volumes were negatively impacted by the alternative lower cost truck market during the third quarter of 2016.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2016 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2015

evidence.text: The decrease in total Transportation revenues was driven by lower rates charged to our customers, including fuel, partially offset by increased volumes in most of our transportation services.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2016 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2015

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed volume trends with increases in some segments and declines in others.

factors.summary: The revenue mix has shown mixed trends, with shifts towards both lower and higher-margin segments. While there has been a shift towards higher-margin services like intermodal and customs, lower-margin segments such as truckload have experienced margin compression, negatively impacting overall margins.

evidence.text: Our intermodal net revenues decreased 24.5 percent to $7.7 million in the third quarter of 2016 from $10.2 million in the third quarter of 2015. Intermodal volumes were negatively impacted by the alternative lower cost truck market during the third quarter of 2016.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2015

evidence.text: Net revenues... increased 1.0 percent... primarily due to decreased costs of transportation, including fuel. Our truckload net revenues decreased 1.8 percent... due to decreased costs of transportation, including fuel. Intermodal net revenue margin increased... due to increased net revenue margins and volumes. Our customs net revenues increased 4.2 percent... due to increased transaction volumes.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2016 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2015

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards lower-margin segments and margin compression negatively impacts overall margins.

factors.summary: The company's market share appears stable, supported by a diverse and unconcentrated customer base. There are no significant gains or losses reported, and the top customers account for a substantial portion of revenues, indicating a balanced customer distribution.

evidence.text: Our customer base is very diverse and unconcentrated. Our top 100 customers represented approximately 29 percent of our total revenues and approximately 29 percent of our net revenues in 2015. Our largest customer was approximately two percent of our total revenues in 2015.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

evidence.text: No specific mention of market share gains or losses.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2016 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2015

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The market share is stable with a diverse customer base, but there are no gains or losses reported.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel, have decreased, which has had a positive impact on margins. However, the company is facing challenges in passing these savings on to customers, as evidenced by the decrease in average truckload rates despite lower fuel costs.

evidence.text: The decrease was due to lower cost of transportation, including fuel, partially offset by higher volumes in most of our transportation service.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2015

evidence.text: In North America, excluding the estimated impacts of the reduction in fuel costs, our average truckload rate per mile charged to our customers decreased approximately 5.5 percent in the third quarter of 2016 compared to the third quarter of 2015.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2016 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2015

evidence.text: The decrease was due to lower costs of transportation, including fuel... Net revenue margin increased... due to decreased costs of transportation, including fuel.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2016 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2015

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Decreased input costs, particularly fuel, have positively impacted margins despite challenges in passing savings to customers.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory shows mixed performance, with compression in gross and operating margins due to decreasing net revenue margins in transportation services, partially offset by slight improvements in gross and operating margins driven by lower input costs and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Our Transportation net revenue margin decreased to 17.6 percent in the third quarter of 2016 from 18.4 percent in the third quarter of 2015. Income from operations as a percentage of net revenues decreased to 37.8 percent in the third quarter of 2016 from 39.5 percent in the third quarter of 2015.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2015

evidence.text: Net revenue margin increased... due to decreased costs of transportation, including fuel... Operating expenses as a percentage of net revenues increased... but net income increased 2.1 percent.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2016 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2015

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Margins show mixed performance with compression in some areas offset by improvements in others.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates a strong differentiation strategy through its global office network and contractual relationships, as well as its technology-driven service offerings such as managed services and small parcel logistics. The evidence highlights growth in these areas, supporting the effectiveness of their differentiation efforts.

evidence.text: Our office network is a competitive advantage. Building local customer and contract carrier relationships has been an important part of our success, and our worldwide network of offices supports our core strategy of serving customers locally, nationally, and globally.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

evidence.text: Other logistics services net revenues... increased 25.5 percent... driven primarily by volume growth in managed services.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2016 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2015

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong differentiation through global network, contractual relationships, and technology-driven services with evidence of growth.

factors.summary: The competitive environment is mixed, with evidence of intensification in some areas and stability in others. While the OVERVIEW section highlights an intensifying competitive landscape due to new entrants and aggressive rivals, particularly in the LTL segment, the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2016 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2015 section indicates no significant changes in competitive dynamics.

evidence.text: In 2015, we worked with more than 110,000 active customers. We work with a wide variety of companies, ranging in size from Fortune 100 companies to small family businesses, in many different industries. Our customer base is very diverse and unconcentrated. Our top 100 customers represented approximately 29 percent of our total revenues and approximately 29 percent of our net revenues in 2015. Our largest customer was approximately two percent of our total revenues in 2015.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

evidence.text: No specific mention of competitive dynamics changes.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2016 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2015

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals with some intensification in competition but no significant changes in dynamics.
